perturbation.original_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA that is identified at the time of disclosure or within thirty (30) days thereafter as being confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA, regardless of whether it is identified at the time of disclosure or not. All information shall be treated as confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The modification removes the requirement for information to be identified as confidential within 30 days, stating that all information is now confidential. This conflicts with the rest of the NDA which establishes protocols and procedures based on the understanding that there is a need for classification.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" as used in this Agreement means all technical or business information disclosed by one of the Parties to another pursuant to the JRA, regardless of whether it is identified at the time of disclosure or not. All information shall be treated as confidential. No information will be regarded as CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION if the Party to which it is disclosed can show by competent proof that such information (a) was at the time of disclosure, or subsequently became, through no fault of the receiving Party, known to the general public through publication or otherwise; or (b) was, subsequent to disclosure to a Party, lawfully and independently received by that Party from a third party who had the right to disclose it without restriction. Specific aspects or details of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be deemed to be within the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because the CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION is embraced by general disclosures in the public domain or in the possession of a Party. In addition, any combination of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION shall not be considered in the public domain or in the possession of a Party merely because individual elements thereof are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party unless the combination and its principles are in the public domain or in the possession of that Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that all information disclosed is confidential regardless of whether it's identified as such, while the contradicted text provides exceptions where information is *not* considered confidential if it becomes publicly known or is received from a third party. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text implies all information is confidential with no exceptions, conflicting with the contradicted text that outlines specific exceptions.

perturbation.original_text: 7. The terms and provisions of this Agreement will inure to the benefit of the Parties, their respective successors and assigns and will be binding on said successors and assigns. This paragraph notwithstanding, neither Party may disclose any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any successor or assign absent prior written consent of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The terms and provisions of this Agreement will NOT inure to the benefit of the Parties, their respective successors and assigns and will NOT be binding on said successors and assigns. This paragraph notwithstanding, either Party may disclose any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any successor or assign without consent of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation inverts the succession clause of the NDA. The original text states that the agreement's terms benefit successors and assigns, and they are bound by it, except for needing consent to disclose confidential information. The modified text reverses this entirely: the terms do NOT benefit or bind successors/assigns, and they MAY disclose confidential information without consent. This makes the agreement far less enforceable and predictable if a party is acquired or assigns its rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. The terms and provisions of this Agreement will NOT inure to the benefit of the Parties, their respective successors and assigns and will NOT be binding on said successors and assigns. This paragraph notwithstanding, either Party may disclose any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any successor or assign without consent of the disclosing Party. 8. The Parties understand and agree that no right or license under any patent, patent application, or know-how is granted to any other Party or any other person by this Agreement or by any disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement on the date first written above. For: NANOLUTION, LLC For: NATURALNANO INC. By: /s/ John Lanzafame By: /s/ Michael Riedlinger ----------------------------- ----------------------- Name: John Lanzafame Name: Michael Riedlinger Title: President Title: President
perturbation.justification: The changed text inverts the succession clause of the NDA, stating the agreement's terms do NOT benefit or bind successors/assigns, and they MAY disclose confidential information without consent. The 'Contradicted Text' is identical to the 'Changed Text,' indicating that the change directly conflicts with itself. This identity confirms a direct, impactful contradiction within the document, making the agreement significantly less enforceable and predictable. The modified text creates an in-text contradiction because it directly opposes the original intent of the agreement regarding successors and assigns, creating a situation where the parties' rights and obligations are unclear and inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The Parties understand and agree that no right or license under any patent, patent application, or know-how is granted to any other Party or any other person by this Agreement or by any disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The Parties understand and agree that a non-exclusive, royalty-free license under any patent, patent application, or know-how *is* granted to the other Party by this Agreement for use solely within the scope of the Project. This license terminates upon termination of the JRA.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that no rights or licenses are granted. The modified text completely contradicts this by granting a non-exclusive license. This creates confusion about what rights each party has to the other's intellectual property, especially since Section 1 also dictates ownership of specific research outcomes. It also clashes with the initial assignment of intellectual property, making it unclear who controls which technologies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. The Parties understand and agree that a non-exclusive, royalty-free license under any patent, patent application, or know-how *is* granted to the other Party by this Agreement for use solely within the scope of the Project. This license terminates upon termination of the JRA. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement on the date first written above. For: NANOLUTION, LLC For: NATURALNANO INC. By: /s/ John Lanzafame By: /s/ Michael Riedlinger ----------------------------- ----------------------- Name: John Lanzafame Name: Michael Riedlinger Title: President Title: President
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits the granting of any rights or licenses related to patents, patent applications, or know-how. The changed text directly contradicts this by granting a non-exclusive, royalty-free license. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document as the changed text creates a clause granting something specifically denied earlier in the agreement. Thus, there is an inconsistency as defined above in the instructions.
